title: I Found Him on a Poster @AWBFilms
text: Bro, we're here somewhere crazy. There's so much security to come here. And it's just a food court here in Karachi. And yeah, we're going to eat rolls. But what is this place called? >> This place is called Creek Drive, and this is in the most elitist area of Karachi. It sure is fancy, man. It's a far cry from Hushe. And today I'm meeting someone for the first time, a good friend, Mr. Arsalan Majid. I met him because I went to Hushe. I rode my motorcycle all the way to where the K2 base camp trek starts. The people I met there, they told me we're in a film. And they showed me the poster, and it was Arsalan's film, Beyond the Wet Lands. And it's like a survival drama about being up in the area of K2. How did you get the locals in Hushe to be in your film? I mean, they were like pretty into it from the start. I mean, they wanted to be a part of it, and because they are like secluded from the rest of the country, they wanted to let everybody know of their presence, that they do also exist. The way to Hushe is like about a 8-hour drive from the nearest town where the airport is. And then it's the temperature there is about -25° in the winter. That's when we went. And when you're shooting a film, it's just way harder for you as a filmmaker to make an actual feature film with local talent who have no prior acting experience. At the end, it's just a really rewarding experience. Go and watch my Hushe vlog where I ride my motorcycle there, then go and watch Beyond the Wet Lands.
